questions.text: What miracle does the first verse of this Surah describe?
questions.answer: The Night Journey (Al-Isra) of Prophet Muhammad (SAW) from Al-Masjid al-Haram to Al-Masjid al-Aqsa.

questions.text: What was the purpose of the Night Journey as stated in Verse 1?
questions.answer: To show the Prophet (SAW) some of Allah's signs.

questions.text: What did Allah decree for the Children of Israel in the Scripture regarding their behavior on Earth?
questions.answer: That they would cause corruption twice and reach a level of great haughtiness.

questions.text: How does Allah describe the Quran's guidance for mankind in Verse 9?
questions.answer: It guides to that which is most upright and gives glad tidings to the believers.

questions.text: What is attached to the neck of every human being on the Day of Judgment?
questions.answer: Their 'bird' (record of deeds), which will be shown as an open book.

questions.text: What is the command regarding treatment of parents in Verse 23?
questions.answer: To worship only Allah and to show kindness to parents, never saying even 'Uff' (a sigh of frustration) to them.

questions.text: How does the Surah characterize those who spend wastefully (the Tabdhir)?
questions.answer: They are brothers of the devils (Ikhwan al-Shayatin).

questions.text: What is the Quranic stance on taking the life of a child due to poverty?
questions.answer: It is strictly forbidden; Allah provides for them and for the parents. Killing them is a great sin.

questions.text: What is the instruction regarding fulfilling promises and covenants (Verse 34)?
questions.answer: Fulfill the covenant, for the covenant will be questioned on the Day of Judgment.

questions.text: What is the command regarding walking on the earth with pride (Verse 37)?
questions.answer: Do not walk with insolence, for you cannot tear the earth apart nor reach the mountains in height.

questions.text: What do the seven heavens and the earth and all in them do regarding Allah?
questions.answer: They exalt Him, but mankind does not understand their way of exalting (Tasbih).

questions.text: What will the disbelievers say when they are raised from the dead regarding their time spent in the grave?
questions.answer: They will think they remained there for only a very short time.

questions.text: How does Allah describe the Quran's effect on believers vs. wrongdoers?
questions.answer: It is a healing and mercy for believers, but increases the wrongdoers only in loss.

questions.text: What is the 'Ruh' (Soul/Spirit) according to Verse 85?
questions.answer: It is of the affair/command of my Lord, and mankind has been given but little knowledge of it.

questions.text: What was the challenge regarding the Quran mentioned in Verse 88?
questions.answer: If mankind and jinn joined together to produce the like of the Quran, they could not, even if they assisted each other.

questions.text: Why did Allah send a human Messenger instead of an Angel?
questions.answer: Because if there were angels walking on earth in peace, Allah would have sent down an angel as a messenger.

questions.text: What is the 'Maqam-um-Mahmuda' promised to the Prophet (SAW) in Verse 79?
questions.answer: The 'Praised Station' of intercession on the Day of Judgment, attained through Tahajjud prayer.

questions.text: How many clear signs were given to Prophet Musa (AS)?
questions.answer: Nine clear signs.

questions.text: What is the instruction regarding the tone of voice during prayer (Verse 110)?
questions.answer: Do not be too loud in your prayer nor too quiet, but seek a way in between.

questions.text: What names should be used to call upon Allah?
questions.answer: Call Him Allah or Ar-Rahman; to Him belong the best names (Al-Asma'ul Husna).
